---
# Previous-row value with windowcomp lag (lead is NOT supported)

`windowcomp lag(<col>) by <partition> sort <dir> <field> as <alias>` returns the PRIOR row's value within each partition (null for the first row of each partition). The `as <alias>` clause is LAST — after `by` and `sort`. Verified on partitioned data: lag yields each row's predecessor by the sort order. `lead()` (next row) is NOT supported — it returns a generic 500; only `lag` exists. IR use: subtract a connection's `_time` from the lag of `_time` per (process, dest) to get the inter-arrival gap — a near-constant gap is beaconing.

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK and action_remote_ip != null
| windowcomp lag(_time) by actor_process_image_name, action_remote_ip sort asc _time as prev_time
| alter gap_ms = subtract(_time, prev_time)
| filter prev_time != null
| fields _time, actor_process_image_name, action_remote_ip, gap_ms
```

_Stage/function syntax live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant (Advanced-auth PAPI, narrow window). The mechanics are confirmed; adapt field names to your dataset._
